title: Rotang crinitus
intro:
There is no information found on the plant Rotang crinitus. However, based on the given name, it seems to be a possible misspelling or variation of the plant name Rauvolfia crinita or Rauvolfia tetraphylla, commonly known as the Indian snakeroot or poison nut. 

If we consider Rauvolfia crinita, it is a flowering plant native to the tropical forests of South Asia. The health benefits of Rauvolfia crinita include its use as an antihypertensive agent, helping to reduce blood pressure, and its potential in treating anxiety, insomnia, and other neurological disorders. The bioactive constituents of Rauvolfia crinita include alkaloids such as reserpine, deserpidine, and ajmalicine, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Rauvolfia crinita include capsules, tablets, and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine systems such as Ayurveda and Unani.
